stories.title: Madrid ATP Semifinal Day — Sinner vs Fils & Zverev vs Blockx

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Sinner vs Fils. 4 PM Madrid.

26 consecutive M1000 wins vs a 9-match win streak. First-ever clay meeting.

One of them goes to the Sunday final with a shot at history.

#MadridOpen #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Belgian wildcard Alexander Blockx — ranked No. 135 — is in a Madrid Masters SF.

He already beat defending champion Casper Ruud this week. Now he faces Zverev at 8 PM.

#MadridOpen #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Today is the biggest day yet at the 2026 Mutua Madrid Open.

The first ATP semifinal goes at 4 PM Madrid time — World No. 1 Jannik Sinner against Arthur Fils. Sinner arrives with a 26-match Masters 1000 winning streak and a chance to win an unprecedented fifth consecutive M1000 title. Nobody has ever done that. Fils hasn't dropped a set on clay all season and is on a 9-match win streak, fresh off his Barcelona title. Their only prior meeting? Sinner won in 2023. This will be their first-ever clay encounter.

The nightcap is Alexander Zverev vs. Belgian wildcard Alexander Blockx (8 PM). Zverev — ranked No. 2 in the world — has everything to lose. Blockx — ranked No. 135 and playing on a wildcard — has already beaten defending champion Casper Ruud to reach his first Masters 1000 semifinal. This is a story that writes itself.

The Sunday final is set for May 3, not before 5 PM. Tennis history could be made — or broken — this evening.

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:30 AM ET
facebook.image_caption: Two semis. One historic opportunity. Sinner, Fils, Zverev, Blockx — Madrid, May 1. Who advances?

article.headline: Madrid ATP Semifinal Day: Sinner vs Fils, Zverev vs Blockx — History on the Line

stories.title: Andreeva Beats Baptiste to Reach Madrid WTA Final

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
A day after turning 19, Mirra Andreeva saved three set points and beat Baptiste 6-4, 7-6(8).

She's in the Madrid WTA final — the first teen to reach three WTA 1000 finals since 2009.

#MadridOpen #WTA #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Down set points in the second-set tiebreak, Andreeva came back to win it 10-8.

A day after her 19th birthday. She's something else.

#MadridOpen #Tennis #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Mirra Andreeva's 19th birthday was April 29. She spent the following day making history.

In the Madrid WTA semifinal, Andreeva trailed in the second-set tiebreak, staring down set point after set point. She saved all three. She then won the tiebreak 10-8 and closed out the match 6-4, 7-6(8) over Hailey Baptiste in 1 hour and 39 minutes.

The result makes Andreeva the first teenager to reach three WTA 1000 finals since the format was introduced in 2009. Seeded No. 9 in Madrid, she now carries a 2026 clay-court record that stands at 12-1.

She meets Marta Kostyuk in Saturday's final at 5 PM Madrid. A first-time WTA 1000 champion will be crowned at La Caja Magica — neither Andreeva nor Kostyuk has won at this level before. Based on this week, one of them clearly belongs at the top of the game.

---

#### Image Caption -- 8:00 AM ET
facebook.image_caption: 19 years old. Three set points saved. WTA 1000 final. Mirra Andreeva is something special.

article.headline: Andreeva Beats Baptiste to Reach Madrid WTA Final — A Birthday Gift Worth Remembering

stories.title: Kostyuk Powers Into First WTA 1000 Final

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Marta Kostyuk is in her first WTA 1000 final.

She beat Potapova 6-2, 1-6, 6-1. 11 consecutive wins on clay in 2026.

She plays Andreeva on Saturday for the Madrid title.

#MadridOpen #WTA #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Marta Kostyuk is in her first WTA 1000 final, and she earned it the hard way.

The 23-year-old Ukrainian had already made headlines before the match began — again refusing a pre-match handshake with Anastasia Potapova for political reasons related to the Russia-Ukraine conflict. On court, she was dominant. Kostyuk beat Potapova 6-2, 1-6, 6-1 in 1 hour and 36 minutes. A second-set blip couldn't knock her off course.

She hasn't lost on clay in 2026. 11 consecutive wins. This is her third final of the year. She beat Andreeva at the Brisbane International earlier this season (7-6, 6-3) on hard court, but Saturday's Madrid final will be their first clay-court meeting.

Saturday. 5 PM Madrid. Kostyuk vs. Andreeva. First WTA 1000 final for both.

---

#### Image Caption -- 12:30 PM ET
facebook.image_caption: First WTA 1000 final. Eleven wins on clay. Marta Kostyuk is ready for Saturday.

article.headline: Kostyuk Reaches First WTA 1000 Final After Beating Potapova in Madrid

stories.title: Sinner Joins Federer, Nadal, Djokovic in Exclusive Masters 1000 Club

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Sinner has now reached the SFs at all nine ATP Masters 1000 events.

Federer. Nadal. Djokovic. Murray. Zverev. Sinner.

Six players. That's the complete list since 1990.

#Tennis #MadridOpen #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
When Jannik Sinner reached the Madrid semifinal, he joined one of the most exclusive clubs in tennis history.

He became only the sixth player since the ATP Masters 1000 series began in 1990 to reach the semifinals at all nine events — joining Roger Federer, Rafael Nadal, Novak Djokovic, Andy Murray, and Alexander Zverev. Six players in 36 years. That is the full list.

But Sinner didn't stop there. He's also now one of just three men in the Open Era to reach the semifinals at all four opening Masters 1000 events of a single season — joining Federer (2006) and Nadal (2010, 2011). And he's chasing something nobody has ever done: a fifth consecutive Masters 1000 title. His last four: Paris, Indian Wells, Miami, Monte-Carlo. Today he plays Fils for a shot at Madrid.

In 26 consecutive Masters 1000 matches, he's lost just two sets.

---

#### Image Caption -- 10:30 AM ET
facebook.image_caption: Federer. Nadal. Djokovic. Murray. Zverev. Sinner. Six players to reach SFs at all nine Masters 1000 events. The exclusive company Jannik now keeps.

article.headline: Sinner Joins Federer, Nadal, Djokovic in Masters 1000 Semifinal History Books

stories.title: Roland Garros 2026 — 17 Days Away and Wide Open

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Roland Garros starts May 18.

Alcaraz is out. Djokovic is doubtful. Sinner arrives as heavy favorite.

Andreeva is the hottest player in the draw. It's wide open.

#RolandGarros #Tennis #TennisFanrecap fanrumor.com

facebook.long_form:
Roland Garros begins May 18. That's 17 days away — and the draw has never been more wide open.

Carlos Alcaraz, the defending champion, is almost certainly absent with a right wrist injury. He has missed the entire clay season. Novak Djokovic has also been absent throughout the clay swing with a shoulder issue. The two biggest names won't be there.

That opens the door wide for Jannik Sinner. If he wins Madrid on Sunday, he'll carry a five-title winning streak into Paris. No one in the Open Era has ever done that.

On the women's side, defending champion Coco Gauff returns to Paris. But Iga Swiatek — a four-time Roland Garros champion — is always a threat. Elena Rybakina is rolling after her Stuttgart title. And Mirra Andreeva? She arrives in Paris as possibly the hottest player in the draw, with a clay-court record and big-match pedigree that no one expected at 19.

The second Grand Slam of 2026 is shaping up to be one of the most intriguing in recent memory.

---

#### Image Caption -- 3:00 PM ET
facebook.image_caption: 17 days. No Alcaraz. No Djokovic. Sinner on a historic run. The French Open draws closer and the field is wide open.

article.headline: Roland Garros 2026 Preview: Alcaraz Out, Djokovic Doubtful, and the Draw Is Wide Open
